title: Houthis Enter Iran-Israel War: First Ballistic Missile Attack on Israel Since November 2025
detail: Yemen's Houthi movement launched its first ballistic missile attack on Israel since freezing cross-border operations in November 2025, marking a major new escalation in the ongoing Iran war. The Yemeni Armed Forces stated they 'carried out the first military operation using a barrage of ballistic missiles targeting sensitive Israeli military sites in southern occupied Palestine.' Sirens sounded across Beersheba and surrounding Negev communities; air defense systems were activated. No casualties from the Houthi attack were immediately reported. The group had earlier shown restraint after the October 2025 Gaza ceasefire, conditioning resumption of attacks on broader regional escalation — Saturday's launch represented a U-turn from that posture. A Houthi spokesman declared: 'Our operations will continue until all our declared objectives are realised... and until Israeli aggression halts on all the resisting fronts.' Analysts warned the entry raised risks of Red Sea and Bab al-Mandab disruption. Separately, an Iranian ballistic missile struck the Jerusalem-area town of Eshtaol — evading air defense interception — injuring at least 11 people and causing extensive property damage in a 150-meter radius; the IAF launched an investigation into the defensive failure.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Yemen's Houthis launch missile attack on Israel as war with Iran intensifies, March 28, 2026
CNN — Live updates: Houthis enter Iran war with missile strike against Israel, March 28, 2026
Jerusalem Post — First Houthi launch toward Israel since war began triggers alerts across the Negev, March 28, 2026
Times of Israel — Several lightly hurt after Iranian missile hits Jerusalem-area town; IDF strikes Tehran, March 28, 2026

title: IDF Drone Strike Kills Man East of Khan Younis; Hamas Operatives Targeted in Gaza City
detail: Israeli forces conducted multiple targeted strikes in Gaza on March 28. A man was killed and several others wounded in an IDF drone strike on a car near the Bani Suheila roundabout east of Khan Younis, close to the ceasefire line on the Hamas-controlled side; the killed and wounded were taken to Nasser Hospital. Separately, Israeli airstrikes killed two Hamas operatives — Fahmi and Saed Qaddoum — in the Shejaiya neighborhood of Gaza City. Hamas's Deterrent Force identified the two as resistance fighters struck after fending off 'collaborationist militia' operatives who attempted to abduct them. The IDF also struck a Hamas arms depot in northern Gaza City and conducted overnight strikes on a weapons production site in southern Khan Younis, citing ceasefire violations by Hamas operatives who fired on IDF troops stationed in the eastern buffer zone. The IDF stated it had eliminated approximately 60 Hamas operatives in Gaza in the preceding month amid continuing low-level clashes.

sources.name: Times of Israel — IDF confirms launching strikes across Gaza after terror operatives fired on troops in Khan Younis, March 2026
Times of Israel — IDF confirms Khan Younis airstrike targeting Hamas operative after attack on troops, March 2026
FDD Long War Journal — Israel continues operations against Hamas in Gaza, diplomats push for disarmament, March 27, 2026

title: UK Hosts International Peace Fund Conference at Lancaster House; Palestine Embassy Opens in London
detail: UK Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper hosted a conference at Lancaster House in London to advance the International Fund for Israeli-Palestinian Peace — fulfilling PM Keir Starmer's pledge to finance long-term Israeli-Palestinian civil society peacebuilding. The conference brought together Palestinian and Israeli civil society representatives to 'build common ground, challenge entrenched divisions, and work towards a future where both states can live side-by-side in peace and security,' drawing on the model of the International Fund for Ireland. UK polling showed 84% of respondents support creating the Fund and 73% want the UK to contribute at least £10 million. The Palestinian embassy in the UK officially opened in London, following UK diplomatic recognition of Palestine in September 2025. Simultaneously, the Palestine Solidarity Campaign held a 'Together for Palestine' march from Exhibition Road, Hyde Park, drawing thousands to central London.

sources.name: UK Government — UK to boost peacebuilding efforts for Israel and Palestine, GOV.UK
